title: Murphy's Bakery Ltd — Project Assessment
subtitle: Full Bookkeeping Cycle — March 2026
intro: Murphy's Bakery Ltd is a bakery supplier registered for VAT at 23% on the bi-monthly system. Complete the full bookkeeping cycle for March 2026 using the information provided. Work through each stage in order — the balances you calculate carry forward into later stages.

transactions.detail: Credit sale to Hotel Plaza — Invoice 201: €800 net + 23% VAT
Credit purchase from Flour Mills Ltd — Invoice FM23: €500 net + 23% VAT
Credit purchase from Sugar Co — Invoice SC11: €300 net + 23% VAT
Hotel Plaza paid their opening balance of €1,230 by bank transfer
Credit sale to City Café — Invoice 202: €600 net + 23% VAT
Paid Flour Mills Ltd opening balance — Cheque 102: €738
Cash sales lodged directly to bank: €400 net + 23% VAT
Credit note CN01 issued to Hotel Plaza — €100 net + 23% VAT (returned goods)
City Café paid Invoice 202 in full by bank transfer

bankStatement.note: Note: Cheque 104 (Rent €800, dated 29 Mar) has not yet cleared on the bank statement.

stages.title: Sales Day Book
stages.intro: Record all credit sales for March 2026 in the Sales Day Book. Cash sales are recorded separately in the Cash Book. Calculate the gross (including VAT at 23%), VAT amount, and net for each invoice.

stages.title: Sales Returns Day Book
stages.intro: Record all credit notes issued to customers in March 2026. A credit note reduces the amount the customer owes. Calculate gross, VAT, and net for each credit note.

stages.title: Purchases Day Book
stages.intro: Record all credit purchases for March 2026 in the Purchases Day Book. These are goods received on credit from suppliers (creditors). Calculate gross, VAT, and net for each invoice.

stages.title: Cash Book
stages.intro: Complete the Cash Book (Bank Columns) for March 2026. Record all bank receipts on the left (debit) side and all bank payments on the right (credit) side. Cash sales are lodged directly to the bank. The opening bank balance is €4,200.

stages.title: Trial Balance
stages.intro: Extract the Trial Balance as at 31 March 2026. Before preparing this, update the Cash Book for the bank charges (€25) found on the bank statement — this reduces your bank balance from €3,838 to €3,813. Enter each account balance in the correct Debit or Credit column. Leave the other column blank.

stages.title: Bank Reconciliation
stages.intro: Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement as at 31 March 2026. Step 1: Update the Cash Book for items on the bank statement not yet recorded. Step 2: Reconcile the updated Cash Book balance with the bank statement balance by accounting for timing differences.

cbAdjustment.label: Less: Bank Charges (not yet in Cash Book)

stages.title: VAT Return
stages.intro: Complete the VAT Return for the bi-monthly period (Jan–Feb but using March figures for practice). Use your Day Book totals to calculate the VAT payable to Revenue. Output VAT is collected on sales; Input VAT is reclaimed on purchases.

lines.label: VAT on Sales (from Sales Day Book)
lines.hint: Total VAT column from your Sales Day Book

lines.label: VAT on Cash Sales (from Cash Book)
Less: VAT on Sales Returns
lines.hint: Total VAT from your Sales Returns Day Book

lines.label: VAT on Purchases (from Purchases Day Book)
lines.hint: Total VAT column from your Purchases Day Book

lines.label: Total Input VAT (B)
lines.hint: Same as Purchases Day Book VAT total
